常見問題：
AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。
AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。
今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。
簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥)

AC Milan Given Chance to Sign Porto's Mexico International Midfielder Hector Herrera

Porto midfielder Hector Herrera has reportedly been offered to potential suitors AC Milan.

27-year-old Herrera has been subject of widespread interest from clubs across Europe over the past few years, and a report from CalcioMercato claims that a summer move to Milan could be on the cards..

The report from CalcioMercato.com hails the box-to-box midfielder as the 'kind of player every coach would like to have available' and says that pace and dynamism are two of his greatest qualities. He is 'the last to give up' and makes his presence felt in both defensive and attacking situations.

Herrera made himself noticed on the international stage back in 2012, when he was a member of the side who won the Toulon Tournament before going on to be a part of the Mexico team who earned the gold medal at the Olympic Games in London. His Mexico team beat a Brazil side that featured the likes of Neymar, Oscar and Marcelo in the final.

The following year, it is said that 'half of Europe' wanted to buy him and he chose to move to Portuguese giants Porto, where he signed a contract until 2019.

One-and-a-half years ago, Italian giants Juventus and Napoli both battled to sign the Mexican international, and Herrera even agreed personal terms with Napoli. However, the move did not materalise and Herrera remained with Porto.

However, it is now come to light that Milan have an interest in the player, although he is 'not a priority' for them. The report from Italy claims that they 'like' the player, however the deal will be-revisited in June when Milan have clearer ideas about the squad they want to build.

同時另一個Herrera

Manchester United transfer report: AC Milan plotting £30m move for out-of-favour Ander Herrera


The 28-year-old has slipped down the pecking order at Old Trafford this season and he is reportedly considering his future at the club.

Milan are keen to bring the Spaniard to Italy and have been tracking him for a number of months.

The San Siro club are ready to make a formal offer this summer and, according to Tuttosport, they believe they can get Herrera for £30m.

Milan hope the midfielder's lack of playing time could play into their hands and they are prepared to drive a hard bargain.

Herrera, who joined United in 2014, will also have only a year left on his contract come the summer.

AC Milan to close refinancing deal with Merrill Lynch: the details

Massimiliano Mirabelli continues his scouting duties while off pitch Marco Fassone is working to secure the financial stability of the club. Negotiations for a new loan are at a closing point.

The Rossoneri club outside the pitch is totally engaged in the refinancing of the debt with Elliott Management Corporation. Between AC Milan (123 million) and holding company Rossoneri Lux (180 million) there are 303 million euros plus interests to be repaid by October 2018 (deadline).

The club CEO Marco Fassone plans to close everything by spring, when he will also have to meet UEFA to talk about the Settlement Agreement. The officials of Nyon want AC Milan to conclude the operation before the meeting, so as to have a clearer view of the Rossoneri’s financial situation. The management is working hard on this front.

There have been several contacts with financial subjects and also important negotiations with various of those. For example, several weeks of negotiations with Hps, ex Highbrige, which offered only 270 million euros. Leaving aside that proposal, even that of the American bank Jefferies, the club needs to find a quick solution. Libero newspaper reveals today that Fassone is about to sign an exclusive agreement with BofA Merrill Lynch.

Marco Giampaolo confessed Milan “put in a strong performance and deserved” to beat Sampdoria. “It’s their best moment of the season.”

The Blucerchiati were beaten 1-0 at San Siro by a Jack Bonaventura strike, although it would’ve been a heavier scoreline if not for Emiliano Viviano’s penalty save on Ricardo Rodriguez.

“We encountered Milan during their best moment of the season,” the Coach told Mediaset Premium.

“They are a team with a lot of technique and, paradoxically, the absence of Franck Kessie made them sharper in their passing. I think tonight’s result and performance owes more to Milan than any errors we might’ve made.

“We cannot compare ourselves to Milan, yet we are level on points with them in the table and will continue to challenge for sixth place, so that is something to be proud of. I couldn’t have asked for anything more other than perhaps a little more sharpness in our passing.

“Milan put in a strong performance and deserved the win. We worked on their wide play in training this week, but they still managed to get round us with their quality.”

Gennaro Gattuso has completely transformed the Rossoneri since replacing Vincenzo Montella.

Galliani: 'Yonghong Li funds checked'

Former Milan CEO Adriano Galliani believes Yonghong Li provided “all the necessary credentials” to prove his financial stability.

There have always been questions around the Chinese investor’s €740m take-over of the club, completed in April 2017, with investigations published by the New York Times and Il Sole 24 Ore.

Today the Corriere della Sera goes so far as to suggest Yonghong Li was already facing bankruptcy proceedings before buying the club.

“Yonghong Li invested €740m to buy Milan,” Galliani told Mattino Cinque.

“We were assisted by a very important advisor and a famous law firm, as was Yonghong Li. Not only did he buy Milan, but another three important things happened.

“First of all, he presented the credentials to the Lega Calcio and was approved. Secondly, the Elliott fund loaned Mr Li over €300m, so they must’ve made their own evaluations.

“Finally, over the summer a transfer campaign worth €200m was completed, giving all the necessary financial guarantees and bank bonds that the Italian rules demand.

“I don’t know the reality of what is happening in China, but one plus one plus one makes three, so that’s how things have gone so far.”

A Chinese bankruptcy tribunal has ordered one of Yonghong Li’s named assets, a packing company, be put up for auction to repay two unpaid debts to banks.

Juve and Milan ask after Florenzi

Juventus could make an offer for Roma icon Alessandro Florenzi and he already turned down Milan, claim Tuttosport.

The versatile Italy international can play as a right-back, winger, midfielder or part of the forward three.

He is the latest player to be born and raised a Romanista, after Francesco Totti and Daniele De Rossi, and wears the captain’s armband when De Rossi isn’t present.

The 26-year-old is only under contract with Roma until June 2019 and, according to Tuttosport newspaper, negotiations are dragging on.

It’s claimed the Giallorossi are trying to save money by offering a slight wage rise and are refusing his demands for a longer contract,

The same newspaper also maintains Florenzi has already turned down a proposal from Milan, but Juventus would be ready to dive in over the summer.

Happy New Year to everyone! These days I spent this traditional holiday at home with my family. In the last forty-eight hours I have seen that some irresponsible news has been reported in the media that have damaged the Club, my companies, my family and myself. Since I bought Milan I have faced every kind of difficulty and endured unprecedented pressure. Following these rumors and irresponsible news, I wanted to create a peaceful environment around the team and society.

We do not understand what purpose these rumors have, but they have now come to the point of damaging heavily the Club, my societies and my family. Therefore I would like to take this opportunity to explain, wishing that it is the last time, that the situation concerning all my personal resources is completely healthy and that both the Club and my companies are working regularly. I therefore hope that no credibility is given to news that is not true but that focuses attention on what is most dear to me and to all of you, namely the management and growth of the team and that you continue to support Mister Gattuso. We will do everything possible to bring the Club back to the top.

This season I watched every game of the team. In the worst moments of the season I have seen many players shed tears for losing a game, as I saw Han Li and Fassone cheer for the winning games. I tried every kind of feeling and emotion. The belief of achieving new successes in the future and giving satisfaction to all the fans that Milan has in the world, means that we endure such great pressures. Thanks to everyone's commitment, the team is currently enjoying an excellent time. Thanks to the efforts of Mr. Gattuso and all the players, the team has had improvements in all respects.

Thank you all.
Finally, for my part and for my wife, I would like to wish you a happy new year, excellent health and success at work.

Juventus look at Bonaventura

Max Allegri has asked Juventus to bring Milan midfielder Jack Bonaventura to Turin next season, it’s reported.

According to Il Messaggero, Bonaventura is just the kind of reinforcement the Bianconeri are looking for.

It would be frankly unlikely, as Milan value him at around €30m and he is a key figure in Gennaro Gattuso’s 4-3-3 formation.

The 28-year-old’s versatility is the key, as he can play in a variety of midfield or forward roles.

He also has an eye for goal, this term contributing six in 30 competitive appearances, plus two assists.

Bonaventura joined Milan from Atalanta for €7m on transfer deadline day in 2014, as Inter gave up their option in the last hour of trading.

Milan wishing a Torino player for next season

The Torino player Daniele Baselli could be in the rossoneri’s agenda for next summer.

Despite the fact that the goal of getting into the Champions League seems almost impossible, and with many doubts about the future due to UEFA’s investigation, AC Milan do not lose hope with their project and thats why they look for player in several clubs like Torino to upgrade their quality.

The more than 200 M € invested in summer have not paid off as expected , although this does not seem to discourage the leaders of the Lombard team.Nor to the rumor mill that strives to be placing new goals on the agenda of the Italian team.

In this case we have, according to CalcioMercato.com, that the Italian entity has been interested in Daniele Baselli.Or at least, they are following his footsteps because this name has been circulating for some time on the agenda of the transalpine team.

It is a 25-year-old midfielder, a key player in a Torino with which he has 22 games (2 goals), and which the Milan team has been spying on several occasions this season. Of course, Milan will not have it easy, as Inter has also show interest on Baselli. A Derby is coming in Milan with Baselli as main protagonist.

Milan in for Dzeko?

Milan will reportedly try to sign Roma striker Edin Dzeko this summer as they look for a ‘guaranteed’ goalscorer.

According to Tuttosport, Borussia Dortmund winger Marco Reus remains the ‘dream’, but Milan also need a striker that “guarantees at least 15-20 goals in Serie A”.

Consequently, Dzeko would have emerged as “a strong candidate”, with Patrick Cutrone, Nikola Kalinic and Andre Silva only managing nine goals between them.

The newspaper explains Roma “need to sell”, and despite the Rossoneri “not having the funds of last summer”, they could afford €25m as Kalinic “is the No 1 candidate to leave.”

Furthermore, the Diavolo would be able to match the Bosnian’s €4.5m salary or even improve it, given Gianluigi Donnarumma is on €6m and Leonardo Bonucci €8m.

Dzeko, who was heavily linked with Chelsea last month, has scored 14 goals in 32 appearances for the Giallorossi this season.
